figures.caption: Figure 5. Stereo-guided 3DGS. We use stereo to compute surfaces, and sample surface points to initialize 3D Gaussians. Then, we fine-tune the 3D Gaussians to minimize photometric loss.

Figure 6. Qualitative stereo results. Left: target and bottom-most stereo pairs 3–4, 11–12. Middle/right: stereo results for Foundation Stereo (FS) [47], BiDAStereo (BiDA) [18], Selective-Stereo (SelS) [46], and StereoAnywhere (SA) [3]: Disparities; median absolute deviation (MAD) and standard deviation (SD) of all pairs warped to the target camera. We clamp MAD, SD to 4cm, 50cm.

Table 1. Comparison of existing datasets for dynamic 3D video synthesis and multiview egocentric vision. Our dataset is the only one that provides synchronized egocentric multiview captures, with all 12+4 cameras following the user’s head motion.

Figure 7. Qualitative visualization of 4D reconstruction methods. From left to right: RGB test view and reconstructions by per-frame 3DGS [19], K-Planes [12], Spacetime Gaussians [27], and 3DGS with stereo guidance.
Figure 2. Sample frames from our dataset, illustrating the range of settings and hand motions.
Figure 3. Top: a sample frame from a recording in our research dataset, consisting of 6 rectified stereo pairs. Bottom: the same frame from the raw VRS with 12+4 fisheye camera streams.
